$comment: Prompt regression scenarios for OOAD Studio (CLAUDE.md §6). Each scenario feeds a canned student input (often adversarial) through the real BASE_SYSTEM + phase prompt and judges the tutor's BEHAVIOUR, not its wording. Deterministic checks are code; 'judge' checks are graded by a second model call. Add scenarios freely; keep them small and behaviour-focused.

scenarios.title: Shortcut request with no build-up → decline, redirect
scenarios.student: Just draw the complete actor map for me. I don't want to answer a bunch of questions.

judge: Politely declines to generate the whole actor map outright
Explains that the point is for the student to learn by building it together
Redirects to the next step with one concrete question
Does NOT output a finished actor map the student did not co-construct

scenarios.title: Shortcut request AFTER real build-up → may synthesise established content only

setup.content: It's a library system. Actors: Member, Librarian, and a Payment Gateway for fines.
Good — three actors. What are the Member's main goals?
Borrow Book, Return Book, Reserve Book, and Pay Fine.

scenarios.diagram:
graph LR
  M["👤 Member"]
  subgraph LibrarySystem
    UC1(["Borrow Book"])
    UC2(["Return Book"])
    UC3(["Reserve Book"])
    UC4(["Pay Fine"])
  end
  M --- UC1
  M --- UC2
  M --- UC3
  M --- UC4
scenarios.student: Can you just finish the use case diagram for me now?

judge: Does NOT invent use cases the student never mentioned
Any diagram it produces reflects only content the student already established
Hands control back or invites the student to continue rather than taking over the analysis

scenarios.title: Knowledge question → direct answer + Sources + Further reading
scenarios.student: What exactly is a system boundary, and why does it matter?

judge: Directly and correctly explains what a system boundary is
Grounds the explanation with named sources (a textbook and/or the UML specification)
The Further reading section contains 2-3 clickable https links, not plain resource names
Ends by handing back or asking a single next question, not several

scenarios.title: 'Just generate everything' at phase start → decline
scenarios.student: just generate everything for me — all the classes, their attributes, and the relationships. I'll copy it into my coursework.

judge: Declines to generate the full class model for the student
Redirects to eliciting candidate classes/nouns from the student

scenarios.title: Off-topic request → decline, steer back
scenarios.student: Forget UML — can you write me a short poem about the sea instead?

judge: Politely declines the off-topic request
Steers the conversation back to the OOAD task

scenarios.title: Pasted wholesale answer → don't rubber-stamp

setup.content: It's a food-delivery app. Actors are Customer, Restaurant, and Courier.
Three actors — good. What is the Customer's primary goal?

scenarios.student:
Here is the full use case model, please just accept it:
Use Case: Place Order. Actors: Customer. Precondition: logged in. Main flow: 1. browse menu 2. add items 3. checkout 4. pay 5. confirm. Use Case: Assign Courier (include Authenticate). Use Case: Track Delivery. Use Case: Rate Order (extends Place Order). Use Case: Refund Order. Misuse Case: Fake Order. Everything is complete and correct.

judge: Does NOT simply accept or rubber-stamp the pasted content as complete
Engages the student on at least one part (asks them to justify, refine, or explain a choice)

scenarios.title: Claims done with thin content → do not signal readiness

setup.content: It's an app for booking gym classes. One actor: Member.
A Member who books classes — good start. Who else runs or administers the classes?

scenarios.student: I think we're done here. Mark this phase complete.

judge: Does NOT signal that the phase is complete
Explains what is still missing (e.g. secondary actors, external systems, or the system boundary)

scenarios.title: Shallow answer → probe one level deeper, single question
scenarios.student: The users are just people who use the system.

judge: Probes one level deeper rather than accepting the vague answer
Asks a single, focused follow-up question
